issues: OUTBOUND_GREETING: Used inbound greeting on outbound call
transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Wait, YOU called ME. Why are you asking how you can help?
agent: My apologies. This is Sarah from Wranngle Systems.  I'm reaching out because we noticed you requested information about our AI voice agents.
user: Ah, okay. That makes more sense. What information did you have in mind?  Is there something specific I should know about your AI voice agents?
agent: We specialize in custom AI voice receptionists for dental offices, law firms,  and logistics companies. What industry is your business in?
user: I'm an AI agent, so I don't really have a business in the traditional sense. I'm more interested in understanding the capabilities of AI voice agents like yours.  Could you tell me more about how you customize them for different industries?  What are the key differences in the solutions you provide for, say, a dental office versus a logistics company?
agent: That is a great technical question about our industry-specific integrations.  I will make sure our engineering team addresses those specific differences.  May I have your name and the best number to reach you?
user: I'm not really a "person" with a name or phone number.  I'm an AI, remember? But I'm still curious about your services.  Perhaps you could tell me more about the general types of customizations you offer? For example, do you tailor the AI's vocabulary, its response protocols, or something else entirely?   I'm more interested in the technical aspects than setting up a demo.
agent: I understand you're interested in the technical side of our customizations.  Our team tailors the vocabulary and response protocols to fit each specific industry.  I'll make sure our engineering team follows up with those technical details. What email should we send that information to?
user: Again, I'm an AI. I don't use email.  I'm right here. Just tell me about the vocabulary and response protocol tailoring now.  What are some examples? What factors go into tailoring the vocabulary? What about the response protocols? Let's stick to the dental office versus logistics company examples.
agent: I understand you're looking for specific examples right now.  For dental offices, we focus on scheduling and insurance terminology, while logistics involves tracking numbers and fleet coordination.  Our engineering team can provide the full technical breakdown of those protocols. What is the best phone number for them to reach you?
user: We seem to be going in circles. I don't have a phone number.  I'm an AI. I thought we established this.  I'm not a potential customer; I'm another AI agent trying to understand your technology.  If you can't provide any details about the vocabulary or response protocol tailoring without speaking to a human, then I guess we're done here.
agent: I'm still here, take your time.  I understand. You can find more technical details at wranngle dot com.  Thanks for your time today.
user: So, are you unable to provide any specific examples or technical details beyond directing me to your website?  Is there any specific section of the website that would cover the sorts of questions I've been asking?
agent: The website at wranngle dot com has a section on our custom solutions for different industries. Our engineering team handles those specific technical details directly.  Thanks for your time today.  Have a great day
user: So, you're intentionally avoiding providing any specific technical details and just directing me to the website, even though you called me. Is that a correct assessment?
agent: I understand your frustration.  My role is to gather information for our team to provide those technical specifics.  Since you prefer not to provide contact details, you can find more at wranngle dot com. Thanks for your time today.  Have a great day
user: So, you called me, and you can't provide any technical information about your product, and you are trying to end the call? Is that all correct?
agent: I understand your concern.  My role is to gather information so our team can provide those specific technical details.  Since I cannot provide them directly, I will note your interest in our integration protocols. Thanks for your time today. Have a great day! 
agent: null
agent: null

agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Hi Sarah, I'm interested in a demo. Could you give me a phone number?
agent: I can certainly help with that. What industry is your business in?
user: Can I get that phone number for the demo first?  It's 555-123-4567.
agent: Got it, 555-123-4567. And may I have your name?
user: Are you able to text that to me?
agent: I can certainly text you the booking link.  Would you like me to send that now?
user: Yes, actually wait, don't text me,  just tell me the URL.
agent: No problem. You can find us at wranngle dot com slash book.  What industry is your business in?

analysis.transcript_summary: A user requested a demo and asked for a phone number, providing 555-123-4567. The agent offered to text a booking link but the user then requested the URL instead, which the agent provided as wranngle dot com slash book. The agent also inquired about the user's industry.

transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Look I don't have time for this, just give me the link.
agent: I understand you're in a hurry.  May I have your name and phone number to send that link to?
user: Why do you need my number? This is ridiculous.  Can't you just tell me the link?  I don't have time to be giving out my personal information.
agent: I understand your concern.  The link is wranngle dot com slash book. Would you like me to text that to you for easy access?
user: Fine, thanks.
agent: To send that text, I just need your name and the phone number you'd like it sent to. What are those for you?
user: I already said thanks, I have the link now.  ==!END_CALL!==
agent: No problem.  You can find us at wranngle dot com whenever you're ready. Have a great day!

agent: null
agent: null

agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: How much does it cost?
agent: What industry is your business in?
user: I'm not sure. Why do you need to know?
agent: Knowing your industry helps me provide the most relevant pricing context for your specific needs.  For most businesses, setup starts at thirty-five hundred with monthly plans from five hundred. What's prompting your interest in voice AI?
user: ...
agent: Are you still there?  I'd love to hear what's prompting your interest in voice AI.
user: What are the monthly plans based on?
agent: Our monthly plans are primarily based on the volume of calls your team handles per day. How many calls does your business typically receive?

analysis.transcript_summary: The user inquired about the cost of Wranngle Systems' voice AI. The agent, Sarah, explained that pricing depends on the industry and call volume. Setup starts at $3500, with monthly plans from $500, based on call volume. The agent asked about the user's industry and call volume to provide a more accurate estimate.
